factors.summary: The company expects a modest capital expenditure of approximately $80 million for the remaining portfolio following the Separation, indicating cautious investment.

evidence.text: For the year ended December 31, 2020, we expect capital spending for our remaining portfolio of businesses following the Separation to be approximately $80 million, which includes approximately $20 million of one-time capital expenditures related to the integration of the ASP acquisition.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company is making a cautious investment with a modest capital expenditure plan.

factors.summary: The supply chain is stable, with no significant disruptions reported during the period.

evidence.text: We did not realize notable increases in loss rates and delinquencies during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020, and given the nature of our portfolio of receivables, our historical experience during times of challenging economic conditions, and our forecasted future impact of COVID-19 on our customer’s ability to pay, we did not record material provisions for credit losses as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: No significant disruptions indicate a stable supply chain, which is beneficial.

factors.summary: Pricing power is evident in some segments with positive contributions to sales growth.

evidence.text: Year-over-year price increases in our Professional Instrumentation segment contributed 1.2% and 1.1% to sales growth during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive contributions to sales growth from price increases suggest strong pricing power.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, which is beneficial.

evidence.text: The year-over-year operating profit margin comparisons were favorably impacted by a favorable sales mix for the three month period.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shifting towards higher-margin segments indicates a positive revenue mix for margins.

factors.summary: Input costs are declining, aiding margin improvements.

evidence.text: The year-over-year decrease in cost of sales during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020, as compared to the comparable periods in 2019, is due primarily to lower year-over-year sales volumes from existing businesses, lower year-over-year material costs, cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, partially offset by the impact of incremental cost of sales from our recently acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: COST OF SALES AND GROSS PROFIT

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Declining input costs are aiding margin improvements, which is positive.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to restructuring and efficiency initiatives.

evidence.text: The year-over-year decrease in cost of sales during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020, as compared to the comparable periods in 2019, is due primarily to lower year-over-year sales volumes from existing businesses, lower year-over-year material costs, cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, partially offset by the impact of incremental cost of sales from our recently acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: COST OF SALES AND GROSS PROFIT

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving productivity from restructuring and efficiency initiatives is a positive sign.

factors.summary: Management is cautiously optimistic, expecting continued improvement in certain areas despite ongoing challenges.

evidence.text: Despite the year-over-year declines in demand from our industrial channel partners, during the three month period ended September 25, 2020 we realized sequential improvement from the second quarter of 2020 and expect this trend to continue in the fourth quarter of 2020.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management is cautiously optimistic, indicating neither strong positive nor negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing challenges from the COVID-19 pandemic and economic uncertainties.

evidence.text: The year-over-year results during the three month period ended September 25, 2020 were driven by increased demand for our industrial imaging products in response to COVID-19 monitoring, electrical grid condition-based monitoring equipment, healthcare product offerings, and safety software as a service product offerings, which was more than offset by declines in demand for portable gas detection instruments, software license and professional service offerings, and demand from our industrial channel partners, all of which were impacted by COVID-19 in both direct and adjacent end markets.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing challenges from the COVID-19 pandemic and economic uncertainties pose near-term risks.

factors.summary: R&D investment is targeted, focusing on key growth areas despite overall reductions.

evidence.text: R&D expenses decreased during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020 as compared to the comparable periods of 2019 primarily due to broad cost reduction efforts, partially offset by targeted investments in key growth initiatives and innovation and incremental expenses from our recently acquired businesses.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Targeted R&D investments in key growth areas despite overall reductions indicate strategic focus on innovation.

factors.summary: New products are gaining traction, particularly in medical and safety segments.

evidence.text: The results during the three month period ended September 25, 2020 were driven by increased demand for our industrial imaging products in response to COVID-19 monitoring, electrical grid condition-based monitoring equipment, healthcare product offerings, and safety software as a service product offerings, which was more than offset by declines in demand for portable gas detection instruments, software license and professional service offerings, and demand from our industrial channel partners, all of which were impacted by COVID-19 in both direct and adjacent end markets.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New products are gaining traction in high-demand segments, driving revenue growth despite challenges in other areas.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are having a mixed impact, with some favorable effects but others offsetting.

evidence.text: Foreign currency exchange rates may adversely affect our financial statements.

The year-over-year decrease in cost of sales during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020, as compared to the comparable periods in 2019, is due primarily to lower year-over-year sales volumes from existing businesses, lower year-over-year material costs, cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, partially offset by the impact of incremental cost of sales from our recently acquired businesses. Year-over-year changes in currency exchange rates increased cost of sales during the three month period ended September 25, 2020 and reduced cost of sales during the nine month period.
evidence.subsection: COST OF SALES AND GROSS PROFIT

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements have both favorable and offsetting impacts, leading to a mixed effect on financials.

factors.summary: The company faces significant geopolitical risks, particularly related to U.S.-China trade relations, which could have a material adverse effect on its business and financial statements. These risks are being actively managed through the Fortive Business System to mitigate supply chain disruptions and drive operating efficiencies.

evidence.text: Trade relations between China and the United States could have a material adverse effect on our business and financial statements.

To mitigate the impact of the economic conditions from the COVID-19 pandemic as well as any escalation of geopolitical uncertainties related to governmental policies toward international trade, monetary and fiscal policies, and relations between the U.S. and China, we will continue applying and deploying the Fortive Business System to actively manage our supply chain and drive operating efficiencies, and continue to collaborate with our customers and suppliers to minimize disruption to their businesses.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant geopolitical risks, particularly related to U.S.-China trade relations, pose material adverse effects on business and financial stability.

factors.summary: The economic environment presents mixed conditions with challenges due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, leading to volatile and uncertain conditions, while the company has managed its liquidity and capital structure effectively despite market disruptions.

evidence.text: As a result of our geographic and industry diversity, we face a variety of opportunities and challenges, including technological development in most of the markets we serve, the expansion and evolution of opportunities in high-growth markets, trends and costs associated with a global labor force, and consolidation of our competitors.

While COVID-19 has created volatility and uncertainty in the financial markets, we have not realized a significant impact on our financial position, liquidity, and ability to meet our debt covenants as of the filing date of this Report; however, we continue to monitor the capital markets and general global economic conditions.

Given the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and current market conditions in the U.S., we have updated our assertion for previously unremitted earnings from 2019 and prior periods due to new facts and circumstances that we did not face in prior periods.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed economic conditions with challenges from COVID-19, but effective management of liquidity and capital structure indicates stability.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong free cash flow generation, supported by positive operating cash flows and effective management of working capital. Additionally, the company holds sufficient liquidity to meet its cash requirements, ensuring financial stability.

evidence.text: Cash flows from operating activities were approximately $1.2 billion during the first nine months of 2020, an increase of $352 million compared to the prior year period.

As of September 25, 2020, we held approximately $1.5 billion of cash and equivalents... We have cash requirements to support working capital needs... As of September 2020, we expect to have sufficient liquidity to satisfy our cash needs for the foreseeable future, including our cash needs in the United States.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong free cash flow generation and sufficient liquidity support financial stability and growth opportunities.

factors.summary: The company faces mixed impacts from interest rate changes. While the phase out of LIBOR poses potential negative impacts, the actual effect on financing costs has been significant, with increased LIBOR rates affecting borrowing margins. However, there is no notable increase in credit losses due to the COVID-19 pandemic, indicating some level of stability in this aspect.

evidence.text: The interest rates on our credit facilities may be impacted by the phase out of the London Interbank Offered Rate ('LIBOR').

From April 24, 2020 to December 31, 2021, the minimum London inter-bank offered rate ('LIBOR') for each of the facilities will increase from 0% to 0.25%, and the minimum base rate for each of the facilities will increase from 1.00% to 1.25%. In addition, with respect to the Revolving Credit Facility and for any Adjusted Four Quarters in which the consolidated net leverage ratio is greater the Unadjusted Maximum Ratio, the applicable margin (as determined based on our long-term debt credit rating) for any LIBOR rate loans will increase from a range of 80.5 and 117.5 basis points to a range of 118.0 and 155.0 basis points.

We did not realize notable increases in loss rates and delinquencies during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020, and given the nature of our portfolio of receivables, our historical experience during times of challenging economic conditions, and our forecasted future impact of COVID-19 on our customer’s ability to pay, we did not record material provisions for credit losses as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Phase out of LIBOR and increased interest rates negatively impact financing costs despite stable credit losses.

factors.summary: The company has been actively managing its debt, including refinancing and repaying loans, which indicates strengthening of the balance sheet. The company is deleveraging, with no significant increases in debt and is managing its balance sheet effectively. Debt leverage is managed through lower average debt balances, reducing interest expenses.

evidence.text: On October 9, 2020, we repaid $350 million of the outstanding $750 million of the 2021 Term Loan. On October 15, 2020, we repaid the outstanding ¥13.8 billion balance of the Yen variable interest rate term loan due 2022 which approximated $131 million.

As of September 25, 2020, we held approximately $1.5 billion of cash and equivalents... We have cash requirements to support working capital needs... With respect to our cash requirements, we generally intend to use available cash and internally generated funds... We also may, from time to time, access the capital markets... Given the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and current market conditions in the U.S., we have updated our assertion for previously unremitted earnings... We have made an election regarding the amount of current earnings that we do not intend to repatriate... As of September 25, 2020, we expect to have sufficient liquidity to satisfy our cash needs for the foreseeable future, including our cash needs in the U.S.
The year-over-year decrease in interest expense in the three month period ended September 25, 2020 was due to lower year-over-year average debt balances.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active management of debt through refinancing and repaying loans indicates a stronger and more stable balance sheet.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory shows mixed results, with some improvements in operating and gross profit margins driven by cost reductions and favorable sales mix, but also faces pressures from lower net earnings and increased expenses, including non-cash charges like goodwill impairment.

evidence.text: Net earnings for the nine month period ended September 25, 2020 were impacted by a year-over-year decrease in operating profit of $88 million, a gain on the sale property of $5 million, and an increase in net interest expense of $14 million associated with our financing activities related to recent acquisitions.

Operating profit margin was 16.3% for the three month period ended September 25, 2020, an increase of 330 basis points as compared to 13.0% in the comparable period of 2019. Year-over-year operating profit margin comparisons were favorably impacted by: Operating expense savings from broad cost reduction efforts, and to a lesser extent, a favorable sales mix, price increases, lower year-over-year material costs, incremental year-over-year cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, and changes in foreign currency exchange rates, which were partially offset by lower year-over-year sales volumes from existing businesses — favorable 160 basis points.

The respective 180 basis point increase in gross profit margins for both the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020 as compared to the comparable periods in 2019 is due primarily to price increases, lower year-over-year material costs, cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, and the favorable year-over-year impact our recently acquired businesses.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed results with some improvements but also pressures.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with sequential improvement observed in the third quarter across all businesses and geographies, but there are also areas where demand has declined year-over-year.

evidence.text: During the three month period ended September 25, 2020, we experienced sequential improvement in demand across all of our businesses and geographies from the second quarter.

Year-over-year sales from existing businesses of field solutions products and services declined at a low-single digit rate and at a high-single digit rate during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020, respectively.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Sequential improvement but year-over-year decline.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated a strong commitment to returning value to shareholders through consistent dividend payments, effective cash management, and strategic debt repayment. These actions reflect confidence in the company's financial health and stability.

evidence.text: On October 9, 2020, we repaid $350 million of the outstanding $750 million of the 2021 Term Loan. On October 15, 2020, we repaid the outstanding ¥13.8 billion balance of the Yen variable interest rate term loan due 2022 which approximated $131 million. On October 14, 2020, we provided a notice that we will redeem for cash all of our outstanding 2.350% Senior Notes due 2021 (the 'Notes') in accordance with the terms of the indenture governing the Notes.

We have cash requirements to support working capital needs, capital expenditures and acquisitions, pay interest and service debt, pay taxes, and any related interest or penalties, fund our restructuring activities and pension plans as required, pay dividends to shareholders, and support other business needs or objectives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong commitment to returning value through dividends and debt repayment.

factors.summary: The company has shown mixed signals regarding its merger and acquisition (M&A) activity. On one hand, it has completed significant acquisitions that are expected to contribute positively to growth, though these come with integration challenges. On the other hand, the company appears to be focusing more on managing its existing operations and cash flow rather than pursuing significant new acquisition activities. The evidence suggests that while there have been notable past acquisitions, current M&A activity is not as active, with a shift towards optimizing current investments.

evidence.text: Net cash used in investing activities decreased $3.4 billion during the nine month period ended September 25, 2020 as compared to the comparable period of 2019, largely due to cash paid for the acquisitions of ASP and Intelex during the nine month period ended September 27, 2019 and the proceeds from the disposition of assets during the nine month period ended September 25, 2020, which was minimally offset by a year-over-year increase in capital expenditures.

The year-over-year effect of acquired businesses, including amortization, and acquisition-related fair value adjustments to deferred revenue and inventory which were less in 2020 than the fair value adjustments recognized in the comparable period in 2019.

With respect to our cash requirements, we generally intend to use available cash and internally generated funds to meet these cash requirements, but in the event that additional liquidity is required, particularly in connection with acquisitions, we may also borrow under our commercial paper programs or credit facilities... We also may, from time to time, access the capital markets... During the nine month period ended September 25, 2020, we provided foreign remittance taxes of $13 million on the repatriation of $310 million of previously unremitted earnings from 2019 and prior periods.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Significant past acquisitions but reduced current activity.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is showing a mixed trend, with some areas experiencing a deceleration while others show improvement. The overall organic revenue growth has been negative, with a decrease in sales from existing businesses year-over-year.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses decreased 0.1% and 7.1% during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020 as compared to the comparable periods of 2019.

Sales from existing businesses in our Professional Instrumentation segment’s Advanced Instrumentation & Solutions businesses decreased at a low-single digit rate during the three month period ended September 25, 2020 and decreased at a high-single digit rate during the nine month period ended September 25, 2020 as compared to the comparable periods of 2019.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative organic revenue growth with existing business sales decreasing.

factors.summary: Volume trends show mixed performance across different regions and product categories. While unit volumes are declining in developed markets, particularly in Europe and North America, there are signs of growth in high-growth markets such as Latin America and China. However, some products are experiencing declines, while others show improvements.

evidence.text: Geographically, year-over-year sales from existing businesses for the three month period ended September 25, 2020 declined slightly in developed markets and increased slightly in high-growth markets, as North America increased at a low-single digit rate, Latin America increased at a low-single digit rate, Europe declined at a mid-single digit rate, and Asia declined at a low-single digit rate, which included growth at a mid-single digit rate in China.

Year-over-year sales from existing businesses of product realization products and services declined at a mid-single digit rate and at a low-double digit rate during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020, respectively.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Growth in some regions offset by declines in others.

factors.summary: The end-market momentum is mixed, with the healthcare and recurring revenue businesses showing resilience, while short-cycle industrial demand and certain industrial sectors face challenges. Medical and safety segments are growing, but industrial and adjacent sectors are experiencing declines due to the impact of COVID-19.

evidence.text: The businesses we believe will continue being relatively more resilient include our businesses with a greater proportion of recurring revenue, including our software as a service businesses that provide critical workflow solutions to their customers, our healthcare businesses, and those with longer business cycles with strong backlogs. We believe our businesses that are more dependent on short-cycle industrial demand and production dynamics will continue to sequentially improve but continue experiencing a challenging environment in the fourth quarter of 2020.

The results during the three month period ended September 25, 2020 were driven by increased demand for our industrial imaging products in response to COVID-19 monitoring, electrical grid condition-based monitoring equipment, healthcare product offerings, and safety software as a service product offerings, which was more than offset by declines in demand for portable gas detection instruments, software license and professional service offerings, and demand from our industrial channel partners, all of which were impacted by COVID-19 in both direct and adjacent end markets.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Resilience in certain sectors but challenges in others.

factors.summary: The company is actively implementing cost reduction measures across multiple areas, including labor expenses, discretionary spending, supply chain costs, and facility expenses. These actions are contributing to measurable savings and margin improvements, with a focus on aligning costs with demand and enhancing cash flow generation.

evidence.text: We plan to continue deploying the Fortive Business System to help drive near-term performance and execute cost reductions in the fourth quarter, where necessary, to align with demand, while emphasizing cash flow generation. These cost actions include reducing labor expenses to better align with demand expectations, reducing discretionary spending, and pursuing cost reductions throughout our supply chain by targeting reductions in both direct and indirect spending and reducing our facility expenses.

Year-over-year operating profit margin comparisons were favorably impacted by operating expense savings from broad cost reduction efforts, and to a lesser extent, price increases, a favorable sales mix, lower year-over-year material costs, incremental year-over-year cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, and changes in foreign currency exchange rates.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active cost reduction measures leading to margin improvements.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to economic uncertainties related to the COVID-19 pandemic, including virus control measures and their impact on supply chains and demand.

evidence.text: Despite the virus control measures in place in geographies critical to our supply chain, we have successfully implemented solutions to support our operations and have not experienced significant production material shortages, supply chain constraints, or distribution limitations impacting our operations as of the date of this Report; however, in light of the uncertainty of the COVID-19 pandemic severity and duration, we are continuing to evaluate and monitor the condition of our supply chain, including the financial health of our suppliers and their ability to access raw materials and other key inputs and may experience shortages, constraints, or disruptions during the remainder of 2020 and thereafter.

Year-over-year operating profit margin comparisons were favorably impacted by changes in foreign currency exchange rates, which were partially offset by lower year-over-year sales volumes from existing businesses.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces moderate risks from COVID-19 related uncertainties, including potential supply chain disruptions and financial instability among suppliers.

factors.summary: Labor costs are being managed effectively through measures such as reductions and furloughs, which have contributed to cost savings and maintained manageable levels despite economic challenges. However, there is no significant impact on margins due to wage inflation or benefit expenses, as indicated by the stability in labor costs.

evidence.text: We did not realize notable increases in loss rates and delinquencies during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020, and given the nature of our portfolio of receivables, our historical experience during times of challenging economic conditions, and our forecasted future impact of COVID-19 on our customer’s ability to pay, we did not record material provisions for credit losses as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic during the three and nine month periods ended September 25, 2020.

SG&A expenses decreased during the three month period ended September 25, 2020 as compared to the comparable period of 2019, primarily due to broad cost reduction efforts that reduced labor expenses to better align with reductions in demand, primarily through the use of furloughs and reductions in salaried compensation costs, as well as other reductions in discretionary spending.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Effective management of labor costs through reductions and furloughs has contributed to cost savings and maintained margins despite economic challenges.
